title: tsdown Official Documentation and Local API Bundle Spike
citation: VoidZero Inc. and contributors. tsdown official documentation, accessed 2026-05-04; npm metadata and OpenClinXR local bundle spike executed with tsdown 0.21.10 on 2026-05-04.
permitted_uses: OpenClinXR TypeScript library and Node/Azure Functions deploy-bundle planning where local smoke tests verify output compatibility.
claims_supported: The official documentation identifies tsdown as a Rolldown-powered library bundler.

The official documentation states tsdown supports TypeScript and JavaScript, declaration generation, multiple output formats, assets, tree shaking, minification, sourcemaps, and Node/browser/neutral platform targets.

The official dependency documentation states package dependencies are external by default and can be controlled with deps.neverBundle, deps.alwaysBundle, deps.onlyBundle, and deps.skipNodeModulesBundle.
The official CLI documentation documents --minify, --treeshake, --platform, --target, --sourcemap, --workspace, --filter, --report, and --fail-on-warn options.
The official docs report tsdown 0.21.10 and MIT licensing as of 2026-05-04.

A local OpenClinXR API spike showed tsdown 0.21.10 can produce a smoke-tested Azure deploy entry at deploy/dist/index.js with a 538.86 kB JavaScript bundle and 1.97 MB sourcemap when application dependencies are explicitly bundled.
A local pnpm dlx spike exposed a transient Node/runtime issue for tsdown under the dlx path, while in-repo package-managed execution under Node 22.19.0 passed the Azure smoke script.
claims_not_supported: That tsdown should replace Vite/Rolldown browser app builds.
That tsdown should replace all package builds before package-level type/declaration needs are evaluated.
That every Azure Functions production deployment path is verified; the evidence is a local import-and-fetch smoke of the deploy artifact.
That pnpm dlx tsdown is reliable on this machine without a Node/runtime workaround; package-managed repo execution is the supported path.
